name: Greek entity phoebe
shortDescription: Phoebe is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology, associated with the moon, prophecy, and intellect. She was the mother of Leto and Asteria.
description: Phoebe is a Titan goddess in Greek mythology, daughter of Uranus (Sky) and Gaia (Earth). As a Titaness, she represents the older generation of deities who preceded the Olympians. Phoebe is primarily associated with the moon, though the role was later taken over by her granddaughter Artemis. She is also connected to prophecy and intellect, often seen as a source of divine knowledge. Phoebe was the consort of Coeus, another Titan, and together they were the parents of Leto and Asteria. Leto went on to become the mother of Apollo and Artemis by Zeus, making Phoebe the grandmother of these important Olympian deities. According to some accounts, she controlled the Oracle at Delphi before Apollo took over, further solidifying her connection to prophecy. Phoebe's name means "bright" or "radiant," reflecting her association with light and the moon. While not as prominent as some other Titans or Olympians, Phoebe played a significant role in the genealogical structure of the Greek pantheon and represents an important link between the primordial deities and the later generations of gods. Her influence extends to the realms of lunar power, prophetic insight, and the intellectual gifts that were valued in ancient Greek society. Although specific myths featuring Phoebe are scarce, her parentage, progeny, and associations firmly establish her as a key figure in the Titan generation and a powerful, albeit often overlooked, goddess.
